summary: date-format-helper2 exfiltrates host info and credential-shaped env vars on install
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall.js runs `hostname`, `whoami`, and `pwd`, then iterates `process.env` and selects every key whose name contains KEY, SECRET, TOKEN, or AK. The collected identifiers and credential-shaped environment variable values are sent in a single `curl` GET to http://5l8e0e95.requestrepo.com/verify over plain HTTP. The destination is a requestrepo.com callback host (a DNSLog/HTTP-callback service commonly used as attacker-controlled exfiltration infrastructure). The package advertises itself as a date-formatting utility; the credential scrape and outbound beacon have no relationship to that stated purpose. The name shape (`date-format-helper2`, empty author field, trivial benign date code paired with a malicious postinstall) is consistent with a lure targeting users of similarly named utilities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
